headline: Assessing Campbell's (CPB) Valuation As Shares Slide And Dividend Yield Climbs
summary: Why Campbell's stock is catching investor attention now Campbell's (CPB) has drawn fresh interest after a prolonged share price slide, with the stock showing declines over the past month, past 3 months, year to date and over the past year. In contrast to that weaker share performance, investors are weighing a business that reports annual revenue of US$10.0b and net income of US$550.0m, split between its Meals & Beverages and Snacks segments. headline: General Mills price target lowered to $32 from $37 at TD Cowen
summary: TD Cowen lowered the firm’s price target on General Mills (GIS) to $32 from $37 and keeps a Hold rating on the shares. The firm lowered earnings estimates and price targets for a number of large cap food makers to reflect the likelihood of higher input costs stemming from the Iran War and limited pricing power, adding that food companies are now prioritizing debt reduction to reduce risk after substantial margin erosion in 2025. headline: 22 S&P 500 Stocks Yield More Than 5%. 6 That Are Worth Buying.
summary: The key is choosing the winners from the losers by focusing on dividend coverage—the cash flow and earnings needed to cover the payouts—and the potential for improving businesses.
